stem: A agricultural research study surveys 25 seeds planted and observes 14 positive outcomes out of 25 (56.0%). The sample contains two subgroups: Group A (8 observations, rate = 50.0%) and Group B (17 observations, rate = 58.8%). The true population composition is uncertain: Group A makes up between 40% and 51% of the population. Estimate the true germination rate and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Simple pooled estimate: 0.56. Weighted estimate (stratified): 0.5522 (range [0.5483, 0.5569]). Combined CI accounting for stratification uncertainty: [0.3707, 0.7333]. Point estimate: 0.5522.

solution_steps: Stratified estimate: weighted by population composition. At w_A=0.46: 0.46×0.51 + 0.54×0.5882 = 0.5522.
common_failure_modes: Using the Wald interval (p̂ ± z√(p̂(1-p̂)/n)) which performs poorly for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
Not recognizing that with n=25, the normal approximation may be poor
Ignoring the population composition uncertainty and using the simple pooled rate
